assessments.rationale: Hydronic heating circuits circulate water or water-glycol mixtures exclusively. Exact media match for sensor capability.
Condensing boilers 55-80 degC flow, non-condensing up to 82-85 degC. Radiant floor 30-45 degC. All within 90 degC limit.
Residential 1-3 bar, commercial up to 6 bar. Safety relief valves cap system pressure. Well within 10 bar.
Boiler circulation 5-30 l/min per zone. Minimum detectable 4.2 l/min (DN20) or 0.25 l/min (DN14) — below operating range.
Closed-loop hydronic systems purged at commissioning. Expansion vessels and auto air vents maintain <0.5% air content in steady state.
Closed-loop hydronic circuits are fully filled and pressurized. Partially filled pipes are a fault state, not routine operation.
Inside boiler casings and compact mechanical rooms, space for 100-250 mm straight run upstream can be constrained. Standard OEM integration consideration, addressable at design phase.
assessments.mitigation: Specify sensor mounting position in OEM boiler hydraulic design to ensure adequate straight run; Integrate flow straightener vane upstream (<$5 BOM cost in volume, adds ~2x DN equivalent); Select DN14 variant for compact boiler platforms (shorter body, reduced absolute straight-run requirement)

assessments.rationale: Hydronic heating operates at 30-85 degC with gradual ramp cycles. NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%. No extreme transients.
